items.title: Lawyers flag legal action over Tiwi Island town manager's decision to block bail of alleged murderer
items.summary: Lawyers prepare legal action against a Tiwi Islands town manager who blocked an alleged murderer from being granted bail to the islands, where his potential return has already provoked rioting.

items.title: Hudson Young finds method in the madness for long-awaited State of Origin arrival
items.summary: It took three years and four series, but when Hudson Young was given the chance to impose his unorthodox abilities on State of Origin football, he didn't hesitate to harness the chaos for New South Wales.

items.title: Investigation finds smuggled drugs cause of inmate Phi Long Dang's death at Fulham prison
items.summary: A 35-year-old inmate at Victoria's Fulham Correctional Centre died of a drug overdose after consuming drugs smuggled into the jail, a coronial investigation has found.

items.title: Lawyers expect will disputes to escalate with over $5 trillion to be bequeathed in coming decades
items.summary: A significant proportion of us believe that an inheritance will be the key to our future financial security, but most families have not even discussed what is in the will, and 40 per cent do not have one at all, a recent survey shows.

items.title: Lawyer says alleged childcare paedophile Joshua Brown will admit to crimes
items.summary: Joshua Brown, who has been charged with 156 offences, including sexual assault, has appeared in the Melbourne Magistrates' Court via video link.

items.title: Cleanaway fined $1.1m, convicted over 2014 double-fatal Adelaide truck crash
items.summary: Almost 12 years after a Cleanaway truck killed two people and injured two others, the company has been sentenced in South Australia's Supreme Court.

items.title: Police arrest man over alleged 1994 armed robbery at Chadstone Shopping Centre
items.summary: A man who allegedly carried out an armed robbery and shot three men at Chadstone Shopping Centre in 1994 has been arrested by police in Queensland.

items.title: Robert Black Farmer refused parole over 2005 attack on Lauren Huxley in Sydney home
items.summary: Robert Black Farmer was handed a 24-year sentence over the attempted murder of Lauren Huxley at her Northmead home in 2005.

items.title: Officers down drone carrying $1 million worth of drugs, syringes into Townsville prison
items.summary: Queensland Corrective Services say the drone was carrying illicit drugs, syringes and a mobile phone.

items.title: Strengthened laws for coercive control, family violence put forward by Tasmanian government
items.summary: New laws designed to strengthen family violence protections in Tasmania include an extension to the statutory limitation period for coercive control offences, and a "nation-leading" levy which would be paid by offenders.

items.title: Darwin Street Art Festival highlights the Top End city's claim as the mural capital of Australia
items.summary: Artists say Darwin has surpassed Melbourne as Australia's street art capital, with work underway on a fresh round of murals across the Top End city.

items.title: Hantavirus cruise ship passengers have WA quarantine period extended
items.summary: Six people who were on a cruise ship that was hit by an outbreak of hantavirus have had their quarantine period in the outer Perth suburb of Bullsbrook extended until June 23.

items.title: National bushfire outlook puts areas of NSW and WA at heightened risk
items.summary: A national bushfire outlook forecasts above-average daytime and overnight temperatures for winter and an increased risk for NSW and WA.

items.title: Housing investors increasingly older, wealthier: RBA
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Man charged over alleged hit-and-run in Buderim that left 13yo boy with critical injuries
items.summary: The young cyclist was riding at dusk when he was hit by a ute that allegedly failed to stop and left him with critical leg injuries.

items.title: Coroner hoping to begin inquest into Hillcrest Primary School jumping castle tragedy in September
items.summary: A coronial inquest into the deaths of six children at a Tasmanian primary school could begin in September, nearly five years after the tragedy occurred.

items.title: Greens likely to play ball with Labor's timeline for capital gains and negative gearing bill
items.summary: Treasurer Jim Chalmers introduced the bill to parliament on Thursday, which the government hopes to pass before the winter recess.

items.title: Dean Solomon 'won't consider' chasing full-time Essendon coaching job, denies reports of Brad Scott rift
items.summary: Despite being Brad Scott's immediate replacement, Essendon interim coach Dean Solomon is not throwing up his hands for the full-time role any time soon.

items.title: Small habits that can lower your grocery bill
items.summary: Small changes to the way we approach our meal planning can make a big difference to the overall cost.

items.title: Man fined for distributing pamphlets targeting MP Allegra Spender prior to federal election
items.summary: A finance executive has been fined $30,000 over unauthorised pamphlets that targeted the Wentworth MP in the lead up to the federal election.

items.title: Ali Khamenei's Iranian home a site of mourning three months after his death
items.summary: Outside the Tehran compound that was home to now-dead Iranian supreme leader Ali Khamenei, hundreds of his supporters gather every night to mourn the only leader many of them have ever known.

items.title: US Justice Department launches criminal probe into E Jean Carroll, source says
items.summary: The probe is focused on whether Carroll committed perjury in testimony tied to two civil lawsuits that she won against Mr Trump — one tied to ‌her allegations that he sexually abused her in a New York department store.

items.title: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi breaks IPL sixes record with stunning knock against Pat Cummins and Sunrisers Hyderabad
items.summary: Australia's captain feels the brunt of the pain as Indian sensational Vaibhav Sooryavanshi continues his phenomenal IPL form.

items.title: A WA charity hay run was in doubt until school students and Lions Clubs stepped in to help
items.summary: Emergency hay deliveries to drought-stricken areas resume after thousands of dollars in donations from school students and charity groups.

items.title: Freshwater development triggers calls for protections of significant trees
items.summary: After several failed court challenges, a council has angered a community with its approval to remove a significant tree to make way for a duplex development, but experts say local governments need to start paying more attention to residents' concerns.

items.title: Four EzyMart Sydney stores among 17 retailers shut over illegal tobacco, vape sale claims
items.summary: The major Australian retail company will part ways with an independent operator after several Sydney stores were shut down for allegedly selling illegal tobacco and vapes.

items.title: Federal government to sue 3M Australia over firefighting foam that contained PFAS
items.summary: The Commonwealth is launching its largest-ever legal claim, suing the manufacturer of a firefighting foam containing PFAS chemicals that contaminated 28 Defence Force bases across Australia.

items.title: Buses 'unsafe and late' in Cairns as regional public transport 'struggles' to match population growth
items.summary: Amid a spate of assaults at bus stops in Far North Queensland, advocates say there's a need for change.

items.title: Hugh Marks denies 'threat' to force ABC news director out of role
items.summary: ABC managing director Hugh Marks says he did not "make threats" to terminate departing news boss Justin Stevens, as senators question his sudden departure.

items.title: Perth mother who lost two children to rare genetic condition wants mandatory tests
items.summary: A Perth woman who lost two children to an extremely rare medical condition calls for mandatory genetic testing for children whose cause of death is uncertain.

items.title: Hamas confirms that Israeli air strike killed its new military leader in Gaza
items.summary: An Israeli air strike in Gaza has killed new Hamas leader Mohammed Odeh, his wife and two of his children, the militant group has confirmed.

items.title: The most influential female composers in history and today
items.summary: From Hildegard von Bingen to Hildur Guðnadóttir, here are some of the most influential women of the music world in history and today.

items.title: French Open handshake drama spices up women's draw as Elena Rybakina eliminated
items.summary: There is no love lost between Tamara Korpatsch and Wang Xinyu after a testy match as Elena Rybakina's hopes of another grand slam title go up in smoke.

items.title: Wanted alleged killer Anthony Pele's social media posts 'incredibly valuable' to police
items.summary: A wanted Sydney underworld figure may have unwittingly helped police after posting social media messages mocking his underworld adversaries from overseas.

items.title: French Open: Alejandro Davidovich Fokina's coach quits via text as Novak Djokovic survives stiff test
items.summary: Alenajdro Davidovich Fokina's French Open comes to an end shortly after his coach flees to Miami after claiming he was unwell after a lunch catch-up.

items.title: Billy Slater refuses to guarantee Reece Walsh State of Origin recall despite Queensland's collapse
items.summary: Queensland's coach bats away questions about the notable absence of Brisbane's star fullback after seeing his side cough up a 20-point lead.

items.title: Legal action filed against federal environment minister's decision to allow land clearing at NT station
items.summary: The federal environment minister's decision to wave through plans to bulldoze almost 3,000 hectares — an area 10 times the size of Sydney's CBD — of habitat, home to threatened species, will be challenged in the Federal Court.

items.title: 'Second wave' of algal bloom forecast for SA's Spencer Gulf
items.summary: Modelling predicts that the algal bloom that has plagued South Australia's coastlines since March 2025 will be moving further through the Spencer Gulf in the next few months.

items.title: Rainy weather drenching Australia's east could turn May into one of the wettest on record
items.summary: A series of low-pressure systems are drenching Australia this week, quickly turning this May into one of the wettest on record for parts of the country's east.

items.title: Stolen Generations brothers share their story ahead of Reconciliation Week
items.summary: Iain Seymour never knew he was Aboriginal until finding out he was adopted three years ago and then meeting his brother Allen Edwards, the son of a woman who played a key part in setting up a poignant memorial to the long-term effects of the Stolen Generations. Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.

items.title: Load limits on Victorian bridges 'wreaking havoc' as councils stuggle to fund maintenance
items.summary: Councils across regional Victoria say they are being forced to ban trucks from some bridges because they cannot afford to fix them to keep them safe.

items.title: Regional employers, academics say one-size-fits-all migration policy will decimate critical workforces
items.summary: Amid ongoing discussion about Australia's immigration intake and migration policies, regional residents say many industries in the bush cannot survive without overseas workers.

items.title: Can farmers become less reliant on synthetic fertiliser? Some are trying
items.summary: A war thousands of kilometres away from Australia's farms is quietly reshaping what crops are planted and what will be available at supermarkets in the future.

items.title: Matthew Perry's assistant jailed for role in Friends actor's death
items.summary: Matthew Perry's live-in personal assistant, who had a central role in the Friends star's descent into ketamine addiction and injected him with the fatal dose of the drug, has been sentenced to three years and five months in prison.

items.title: One in 10 Australian teens relying on withdrawal method and half not using condoms as STIs rise
items.summary: A long-running survey of Australian teens has identified falling condom use and unwanted sex as major concerns for young people starting relationships.

items.title: Upper house member Ruth Forrest calls for apology after personal health request leaked to media
items.summary: A Tasmanian independent politician is calling for an apology after her personal health-related request via email to the government was leaked to a media outlet.

items.title: Torquay artist's willie wagtail paintings taking flight around the Surf Coast and beyond
items.summary: A Victorian artist paints hundreds of willie wagtails a week to give away locally and now they're popping up all over the world.

items.title: State of Origin: Ethan Strange and James Tedesco star in Blues' 22-20 win over Maroons
items.summary: When the Blues needed heroes to get on top of the Maroons in State of Origin I, they turned to a debutante and the oldest player on the field.

items.title: Australia ranks third in the world for battery capacity and WA is leading the charge
items.summary: Western Australia in particular is proving to the world that grid-scale batteries are no longer a complementary technology but can play a central role in power grids.

items.title: Labor is quietly confident the battery boom will help prevent power price shocks
items.summary: Within the government, there's quiet confidence the battery boom will continue to gather pace and help shield Australia from both global shocks and domestic demand surges, driving prices even lower.

items.title: Israel warns all residents in southern Lebanon to leave
items.summary: It is the first time Israel has made such a sweeping demand since US President Donald Trump announced in April a ceasefire had been imposed in Lebanon.

items.title: Explain the Internet: The Backrooms captured cyberspace and now it's infecting cinemas
items.summary: Forget about slashers and body horror — the next evolution of fright stems from an fluorescent-lit room.

items.title: Put your Australian TV show knowledge to the test with this picture quiz
items.summary: How well do you know your Neighbours from your Deadloch? Take our single-frame Australian TV quiz to find out.

items.title: New York and New Jersey investigate 2026 FIFA World Cup ticket prices
items.summary: In a joint statement, New Jersey's attorney-general accused the football governing body of having turned "buying a ticket to the World Cup into a gauntlet of confusion, fake scarcity, and impossibly high prices".

items.title: Trump says US 'not satisfied' with Iran peace deal proposals
items.summary: It comes as the White House rejects a draft deal being reported by Iranian state TV as a "fabrication".

items.title: State of Origin: Kalyn Ponga shattered by send-off in Game I as Ashley Klein overrules bunker
items.summary: Kalyn Ponga is left feeling responsible for Queensland's Origin I collapse as the decision to send him off leaves several greats shocked.

items.title: State of Origin quick hits: James Tedesco scores Blues' match-winner, Maroons star Kalyn Ponga sent off
items.summary: The Blues leave it late, the Maroons finish with 12 players, while the bunker draws the ire of players, commentators and spectators alike. Here are five quick hits from State of Origin I.

items.title: Rescuers find villagers trapped in Laos cave after landslide
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Five people trapped in flooded cave in Laos found alive but two still missing
items.summary: Five of the seven people, who entered the cave in central Laos more than a week ago, have been found alive. Rescuers are still searching for two.

items.title: NASA reveals plans for living on the moon
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: NT police say cause of Kumanjayi White's death in custody in Alice Springs still unknown
items.summary: The Northern Territory police commissioner says the cause of 24-year-old Kumanjayi White's death in custody remains undetermined, with the criminal case against the officers who restrained him now closed.

items.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
items.summary: Latest trusted news from the world of finance and business.

items.title: Death in custody of Patience Kuzmins leaves family heartbroken, inquest hears
items.summary: The family of a young Indigenous woman who died in a NSW jail has told an inquest that she was a talented artist who generously supported anyone in need. The inquest is examining Corrective Services NSW's search and body scan practices.

items.title: 'Optimism' as Walk for Truth ends despite no formal commitment from PM
items.summary: The Walk for Truth ended at Parliament House today and while there was not a firm commitment from the PM, many felt the response was a 'positive move'.

items.title: Whyalla locals call for more certainty around steelworks' future amid news of final bidders
items.summary: Whyalla locals welcome more certainty about the future ownership of the city's steelworks, but say it is still not enough to warrant business investment and workers coming back to the Spencer Gulf.
